questions.question: Explain the scope resolution operator (::) and list its various uses in C++.
keyPoints: :: is a unary operator for accessing entities in different scopes
Used to access global variables when local variables have same name
Used to define member functions outside class definition
Used to access static class members
Used with namespaces (std::cout)
Used with enum class constants
Used in inheritance to access base class members
Used to access nested classes

questions.question: How does the scope resolution operator help in resolving naming conflicts? Give an example.
keyPoints: Naming conflicts occur when same name exists in different scopes
:: allows explicit specification of which scope to use
Example: global int x=10; local int x=5; use ::x to access global
Example: class has func(); global has func(); use ::func() for global
Eliminates ambiguity by qualifying names with their scope

questions.question: Differentiate between the dot operator (.) and scope resolution operator (::) with examples.
keyPoints: Dot operator (.) accesses members through object instance
Scope resolution (::) accesses members by scope name
Example: obj.member uses ., ClassName::member uses ::
:: is for static/class members, . is for instance members
:: works without objects, . requires objects

questions.question: Write a C++ program demonstrating how to access a global variable when a local variable with the same name exists.
keyPoints: Declare global variable outside main
Declare local variable with same name inside main/function
Use ::variableName to access global variable
Show both local and global values using cout
Demonstrate that :: overrides local scope

questions.question: Explain how :: is used with enum class. Why is it necessary?
keyPoints: enum class creates strongly typed enumerations
Enum values are not implicitly converted to integers
Values are scoped to enum type name
Must use EnumName::Value to access enum constants
Prevents naming conflicts with other enum values
Example: Color::Red, not just Red

questions.question: How is the scope resolution operator used in inheritance? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Used to access base class members from derived class
Useful when derived class overrides base class methods
Syntax: BaseClass::memberName
Allows calling base class version when overridden
Example in constructor: Derived() : Base::member() {}

questions.question: Write a C++ class with a member function defined outside the class using :: operator.
keyPoints: Class declaration with function prototype
Function definition using ClassName::functionName()
Shows proper syntax of :: for outside definitions
Demonstrates access to class members inside function
Explains why outside definition is needed for large functions

questions.question: Explain in detail all the different contexts where the scope resolution operator (::) is used in C++. Provide appropriate code examples for each context.
keyPoints: Global scope access: ::variableName for global variables
Class scope: ClassName::member for static/class members
Namespace access: std::member for standard library
Enum class: EnumName::value for scoped enumerations
Member function definition: ClassName::func() outside class
Inheritance: BaseClass::member for base class access
Nested classes: OuterClass::InnerClass
Template nested types: TemplateClass<T>::type
Static member definition: int ClassName::variable
Operator overloading: ClassName::operator+

questions.question: Create a C++ program that demonstrates: (i) Accessing global and local variables with same name (ii) Defining a class member function outside class (iii) Using enum class (iv) Accessing base class member in inheritance.
keyPoints: Program shows global variable x and local x, uses ::x for global
Class with member function defined outside using ::
Enum class demonstration with Color::Red
Base class with display(), derived overrides, calls Base::display()
All concepts integrated in single coherent program
Proper output demonstrating each concept

questions.question: Explain the significance of std:: in modern C++ and compare it with 'using namespace std'. Also explain why scope resolution is preferred.
keyPoints: std is the standard namespace containing library components
std:: explicitly qualifies each name from namespace
using namespace std brings all names into global scope
std:: avoids naming conflicts
using namespace can cause ambiguity with user-defined names
Best practice: explicitly use std:: for clarity
Shows professional coding standards
Demonstrates understanding of namespace pollution

questions.question: Write a comprehensive C++ program demonstrating the use of :: operator with static members, nested classes, and operator overloading.
keyPoints: Static member variable definition using ClassName::var
Static member function call using ClassName::func()
Nested class access using Outer::Inner
Operator overloading as member: ReturnType ClassName::operator+(params)
Complete working program showing all concepts
Output demonstrating each use case
Explanation of why :: is necessary in each case
